description: Build a browser-native TypeScript port of `BBC Master 128 Elite (Acornsoft)` that preserves gameplay behavior and validates deterministic outputs against a `jsbeeb` reference harness.

userStories.title: Freeze the reference contract
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the canonical machine, disc, corpus, and phase-1 non-goals captured in docs so that all later work targets one correctness contract.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/elite-typescript-port-step-by-step.md` remains the active runbook for the port
`docs/tasks/reference-contract.md` records the canonical machine `BBC Master 128 (DFS)`, canonical disc `elite-master-sng47.ssd`, reference corpus, exploratory artifacts, and phase-1 non-goals
The reference contract explicitly states that implementation work starts only after the contract is written

userStories.title: Create the TypeScript toolchain scaffold
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want package and build config in place so that browser code, tests, and harness scripts share one TypeScript toolchain.
acceptanceCriteria: Create root `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `vite.config.ts`, `vitest.config.ts`, and `index.html` for the browser port

userStories.title: Create the browser shell and test layout
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want a minimal browser shell and stable test directories so that runtime code, UI code, and harness code have clear homes before subsystem work starts.
acceptanceCriteria: The browser entry renders a placeholder game shell without pulling reference-harness code into the browser bundle
Node version alignment with `tools/jsbeeb/package.json` is documented or enforced
`npm run test` starts the test runner

userStories.title: Boot the `jsbeeb` reference session
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want a deterministic reference boot path so that later fixture extraction and parity tests start from one known emulator session contract.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/reference/boot-reference.ts` boots `BBC Master 128 (DFS)` with the SNG47 disc and confirms the expected session contract
The session bootstrap exposes a typed result that later harness helpers can reuse

userStories.title: Add control, breakpoint, and memory helpers to the reference harness
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want deterministic execution helpers around the emulator so that fixture capture can stop, inspect, and mutate reference state reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/reference/breakpoints.ts` and extend the session helpers with run-for-cycle, run-until-address, breakpoint registration, memory read/write, and key input helpers
The harness can stop on a breakpoint and read or write memory deterministically within one session
Key input helpers can drive curated boot or gameplay setup flows needed by fixture extraction

userStories.title: Add capture and snapshot helpers to the reference harness
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want screenshot, text, and snapshot helpers so that later stories can extract deterministic evidence instead of relying on manual emulator sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: The harness can capture text output, screenshots, and snapshots, and can restore a snapshot into a repeatable session
`tests/reference/reference-harness.test.ts` verifies boot, capture, breakpoint, and restore flows

userStories.title: Define fixture manifest and capture conventions
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want one fixture schema and directory layout so that every later subsystem proves correctness the same way.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture locations are defined for `rng`, `galaxy`, `market`, `text`, `movement`, `tactics`, `commander`, `screens`, and `scenes`
The fixture record schema includes the reference disc and model, source routine or routines, setup method, exact inputs, exact expected outputs or image, and the capture script used

userStories.title: Add fixture extraction and scene capture scripts
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want reusable extraction scripts so that deterministic fixtures and visual scenes can be refreshed without manual steps.
acceptanceCriteria: The scripts write reference and visual fixture records that follow the manifest schema and land in the defined fixture directories
One deterministic fixture extraction and one scene capture can be produced successfully from the scripts

userStories.title: Implement fixed-width arithmetic and bit helpers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want fixed-width arithmetic helpers so that JavaScript number semantics do not leak into gameplay logic.
acceptanceCriteria: The helpers cover u8 and u16 masking, carry and borrow helpers, rotate and shift behavior, signed and unsigned interpretations, and sign-magnitude encode and decode
Unit tests cover wraparound and byte-level semantics used by RNG, movement, combat, and saves

userStories.title: Model workspace and ship block state
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want typed workspace and ship block state so that early simulation code can preserve original semantics without opaque memory blobs as the main API.
acceptanceCriteria: State objects expose named fields and support lossless conversion to and from the byte layouts needed by fixtures
Unit tests round-trip workspace and ship block structures through byte layouts

userStories.title: Add commander state serialization
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want typed commander state and serializers so that save data and fixture state can be consumed without hand-editing byte arrays.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/core/state/commander/commander-state.ts`, `src/core/state/serialization/`, and commander state tests under `tests/unit/state/`
Commander state serializes and deserializes losslessly to the byte layouts required by early gameplay systems
Tests verify round-trip serialization and the full set of commander bytes needed by the first subsystems

userStories.title: Port ship blueprint and lookup-table data
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want typed static ship and lookup data so that movement, combat, and rendering code can depend on reference-matched tables without assembler-era runtime structures.
acceptanceCriteria: The modules contain reference-matched ship blueprint and lookup data required by movement, combat, and rendering subsystems
Tests compare blueprint counts, key fields, and lookup table bytes against extracted reference fixtures

userStories.title: Port market, token, dashboard, and asset data
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the remaining static data expressed as typed modules and assets so that gameplay and UI systems can consume reference data directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Market tables, text tokens, dashboard data, and related assets are represented as typed modules or assets instead of assembler-era runtime structures
Tests compare market, token, and dashboard reference data against extracted fixtures where applicable

userStories.title: Port RNG core and galaxy seed handling
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want exact RNG and seed logic so that later galaxy, market, and simulation systems can reproduce reference outputs deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: RNG stepping and galaxy seed transforms produce exact reference outputs for known fixtures
`tests/reference/rng.test.ts` covers deterministic sequences and seed transitions

userStories.title: Port system generation and naming
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want system generation and name creation ported exactly so that the browser runtime reproduces the same galaxies as the reference build.
acceptanceCriteria: Known galaxy seeds produce reference-matched economies, governments, tech levels, inhabitants, and system names
`tests/reference/galaxy.test.ts` covers representative system records and name generation

userStories.title: Port market generation
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want deterministic market generation so that docked trade flows and status displays can match reference prices and availabilities.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated prices and availabilities match captured reference fixtures for representative systems and commander states

userStories.title: Implement vector, angle, and projection helpers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want deterministic geometry helpers so that later ship, planet, sun, and scanner renderers can depend on stable reference-matched math.
acceptanceCriteria: Projection math matches isolated reference fixtures before full-scene renderers are written
`tests/reference/projection.test.ts` covers vector transforms, angle calculations, and screen projection outputs

userStories.title: Implement rasterizer, pixel plotter, and circle primitives
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want low-level drawing primitives ported in isolation so that higher-level renderers can be verified against stable pixel behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Drawing primitives emit reference-matched pixels for captured isolated scenarios
`tests/reference/rasterizer.test.ts` covers lines, points, and circles against fixture outputs

userStories.title: Model local universe state and ship spawning
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want typed local-universe structures and spawn logic so that the runtime can build the nearby simulation bubble with reference semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Local bubble state can represent active objects and spawn curated reference scenarios without opaque memory blobs
`tests/reference/local-bubble.test.ts` covers spawn results and local-bubble slot allocation for captured scenarios

userStories.title: Port ship movement, orientation, and scanner state
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want reference-matched movement and scanner logic so that the local simulation bubble evolves exactly like the original game.
acceptanceCriteria: Captured scenarios produce the same position, orientation, velocity, and scanner-state transitions as the reference harness
`tests/reference/movement.test.ts` covers representative motion and orientation parity cases

userStories.title: Port tactics decision logic
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want tactical decision rules ported faithfully so that NPC behavior in the TypeScript runtime matches Elite rather than a loose approximation.
acceptanceCriteria: Captured encounter fixtures produce the same tactical decisions, target-selection changes, and AI flags as the reference machine
`tests/reference/tactics.test.ts` covers representative hostile, police, trader, and docking-adjacent cases

userStories.title: Port combat systems
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want laser and missile behavior ported exactly so that combat outcomes in the browser runtime match the reference machine.
acceptanceCriteria: Combat fixtures match reference hit resolution, missile arming and locking, and damage state transitions
`tests/reference/combat.test.ts` covers representative laser and missile scenarios

userStories.title: Port collision and cargo scooping
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want close-range collision and scooping behavior ported accurately so that immediate interactions update world state exactly like the reference build.
acceptanceCriteria: Collision and scooping fixtures match reference collision outcomes, cargo pickup rules, and inventory updates
`tests/reference/cargo.test.ts` covers representative collision and scooping flows

userStories.title: Port docking checks and docking computer
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want docking validation and auto-docking behavior ported faithfully so that docking feels like the reference game.
acceptanceCriteria: Docking fixtures match reference clearance, approach, and auto-docking behavior for curated scenarios
`tests/reference/docking.test.ts` covers manual docking checks and docking-computer flows

userStories.title: Port the main flight loop
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want a readable TypeScript flight loop so that the port replaces the reference orchestration path without losing observable behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Stepped flight-loop captures for combat, docking, planet approach, and ordinary travel agree with `jsbeeb`
The flight-loop code stays modular and readable while preserving reference ordering and observable effects

userStories.title: Port the main game scheduler and spawn rules
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the top-level game scheduler ported so that the runtime can enter play, schedule events, and change major view state in the same order as the reference build.
acceptanceCriteria: The top-level loop can enter play, schedule events, spawn game events, and transition between high-level view states in reference order
`tests/reference/main-game-loop.test.ts` covers representative scheduler and spawn sequences

userStories.title: Port docked economy and equipment flows
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want docked trade and equipment systems ported so that the browser version can change cargo, credits, and equipment with reference behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Buying, selling, and equipment changes produce reference-matched credits, cargo, legal status, and equipment state
`tests/reference/economy.test.ts` covers representative trade and equipment flows

userStories.title: Port galaxy map and hyperspace logic
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want navigation and jump logic ported so that travel through the galaxy matches the reference runtime exactly.
acceptanceCriteria: Map calculations, hyperspace setup, countdown, jump results, and arrival state match captured reference fixtures
`tests/reference/navigation.test.ts` covers representative local and galactic jump flows

userStories.title: Port mission state progression
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want mission state tracked explicitly so that scripted progression and rewards remain faithful to the reference build.
acceptanceCriteria: Mission triggers, flags, and reward or state transitions match captured reference scenarios

userStories.title: Port commander save/load
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want commander save and load logic ported so that browser persistence and reference fixtures both use the same typed format.
acceptanceCriteria: Commander saves serialize to the expected byte layout and load back into equivalent typed state
`tests/reference/commander.test.ts` covers save and load round trips plus fixture-matched save bytes

userStories.title: Port token expansion and text formatting
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the text token and formatting layer ported so that scripted text no longer depends on screen-memory-era printing logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Scripted text expansion and formatting outputs match reference fixtures for market, status, and mission text cases
`tests/reference/text.test.ts` covers representative token and formatting cases

userStories.title: Port goat soup and generated text
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want generated text ported exactly so that system descriptions and similar prose match the reference build.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated system descriptions and other goat-soup outputs match captured reference fixtures for representative systems
`tests/reference/text.test.ts` includes goat-soup cases for multiple systems

userStories.title: Build the Canvas2D renderer foundation
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want a clean browser rendering boundary so that the game can draw in the browser now and still support a future renderer swap later.
acceptanceCriteria: The renderer boundary keeps simulation and presentation code independent from Canvas2D-specific details
Framebuffer output can be drawn to a browser canvas deterministically in tests

userStories.title: Implement ship, planet, sun, and starfield renderers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the main space-scene renderers in place so that the browser build can show stable reference-matched world visuals.
acceptanceCriteria: Bounded scene fixtures render stable images that satisfy the visual regression threshold against reference captures
`tests/visual/space-scenes.test.ts` covers representative ship, planet, sun, and starfield scenes

userStories.title: Implement scanner, dashboard, and sights renderers
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the cockpit presentation layer ported so that the browser build can show the same scanner, dashboard, and sight information as the reference game.
acceptanceCriteria: Cockpit-scene fixtures render stable scanner, dashboard, and sight outputs that satisfy the regression threshold
`tests/visual/cockpit-scenes.test.ts` covers representative cockpit layouts and UI overlays

userStories.title: Add browser input adapters
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want browser input adapters so that keyboard and optional joystick control can drive the runtime in a browser-native way.
acceptanceCriteria: The browser input layer maps keyboard controls and optional joystick inputs into the runtime command model
`tests/integration/browser-input.test.ts` covers representative key and joystick interactions in the browser shell

userStories.title: Add browser audio and commander storage
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want browser audio and storage adapters so that the browser build can play cues and persist commander data without emulator internals.
acceptanceCriteria: The browser adapters play core audio cues and persist commander data through the defined storage boundary
`tests/integration/browser-platform.test.ts` covers audio trigger wiring and commander save persistence

userStories.title: Integrate the game shell, HUD, and shared UI chrome
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the browser shell to compose runtime and UI dependencies cleanly so that later screen flows can reuse one shared integration point.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `src/app/game-shell.ts` and add shared modules under `src/ui/hud/` or `src/ui/components/`
The shell composes runtime, renderer, input, audio, and storage boundaries without importing `jsbeeb` harness code into the browser bundle
Shared HUD and UI modules render the common flight and docked chrome needed by later screen flows
`tests/integration/browser-shell.test.ts` covers shell lifecycle and dependency wiring

userStories.title: Integrate bootstrap and title/loading flow
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want startup and title flow integrated so that the browser build behaves like a product instead of a set of disconnected subsystems.
acceptanceCriteria: Browser startup boots into a usable loading-to-title flow while preserving the title-screen and hangar feel without reproducing delivery-medium copy-protection choreography

userStories.title: Integrate flight and docked screens
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want the main user-facing screens connected so that the browser build can move cleanly between title, flight, and docked play states.
acceptanceCriteria: The browser shell can enter gameplay, return to docked flow, and route between title, flight, and docked screens from game state
`tests/integration/screen-flow.test.ts` covers representative transitions into flight and docked views

userStories.title: Expand automated acceptance evidence
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want broad deterministic and visual evidence suites so that the port is proven mechanically before final manual playtesting.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated acceptance checks cover deterministic core-simulation fixtures, visual regression scenes for dashboard, scanner, ships, sun, planet, title, and loading screens, save and load round trips, and end-to-end flows for launch, docking, trade, hyperspace, combat, death, and save or load
The acceptance suites run successfully in CI-friendly mode

userStories.title: Document playtesting and fidelity-gap tracking
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want manual playtest guidance and a fidelity-gap ledger so that experiential differences are tracked alongside automated evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: The playtest checklist covers input feel, movement feel, docking feel, combat feel, audio feel, pacing, and readability
Every known fidelity gap is either fixed, explicitly accepted, or recorded with a clear follow-up note

userStories.title: Package, document, and ship the browser port
userStories.description: As a port engineer, I want packaging, documentation, and CI artifacts completed so that another engineer can build, test, and understand the browser port end to end.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update the root `README.md`, `docs/tasks/release-checklist.md`, browser build output config, and CI scripts for build and test flows
Documentation explains the reference corpus, legal constraints, verification harness, and the relationship between the TypeScript port and the reference build
The repository exposes one command each for local development, the full test suite, reference extraction refresh, and production build
CI runs the documented build and test commands successfully
